meta.methodology: For each R1 slot in 2024 and 2025, we estimate the pre-draft consensus probability that the team would actually make the pick they made. Consensus probabilities are approximated from archived NFLMockDraftDatabase snapshots in the final 48 hours before the draft, cross-checked against PFF/ESPN/Walter Football/Kiper recap articles identifying surprises vs. chalk.
meta.source_notes: NFLMockDraftDatabase pre-draft consensus (final week), PFF 2024 & 2025 first-round grades (what-we-got-wrong sections), CBS Sports 'Five things experts got wrong' 2025 recap, ESPN Kiper recap, Yahoo 'surprises' columns, The Ringer post-draft analysis, B/R big-board deltas. Consensus percentages are bucket-level estimates (80+, 60-80, 35-60, <35) since exact per-analyst percentages vary by source.
meta.bucket_granularity: Estimates are rounded to bucket-level (not exact per-mock percentages). This is deliberate — the calibration question is about bucket hit rates, not point estimates.

consensus_80plus.description: Pre-draft consensus >= 80% — 'chalk' picks everyone saw coming

consensus_35_60.hits_to_misses_note: Above 20 cases includes 10 clean hits at top-of-bucket (50-60%) and 10 misses at bottom-of-bucket (35-45%) where the modal pick did not happen. Examples of misses in this bucket not listed above: slot 20 PIT where Harmon was a late riser over cluster alternatives; slot 22 LAC where Hampton beat modal CB expectations; slot 12 DAL, slot 13 MIA, slot 14 IND (2025) where modal picks (Banks-fall, Warren, Harmon) missed; slot 24 MIN, slot 28 DET, slot 29 WAS where interior OL vs. DL coin-flips went off-modal; slot 26 LAR where Pearce was a reach vs. modal; slot 29 WAS Conerly over modal CB. Net: ~10/20 hit rate.

consensus_under_35.hits_to_misses_note: 28 cases, 6 clean hits. Most 'hits' were narrow modal picks barely under 35% (Williams, Booker, Grant, Barron, Warren). Most misses were genuine surprises — Penix at 8, Pearsall at 31, Stewart at 17, Zabel at 18, Dart at 25, Pearce at 26, Loveland at 10 over Warren.

aggregate_stats.note: Majority of R1 slots (44%) have NO strong modal consensus pick — this is the baseline difficulty of the problem.

current_model_thresholds.problem: HIGH threshold (0.60) maps to empirical ~80-100% hit rate — overconfident label not because the label is wrong but because 60%+ modal probability in a true-consensus environment is VERY rare. MEDIUM bucket (0.35-0.60) actually hits only ~50% empirically, which is defensible. LOW bucket (<0.35) hits ~21% empirically — the label is honest but the bucket is too wide; a 30% modal pick is materially more likely than a 15% modal pick.

recommended_thresholds.rationale: Empirics show a clean step function at 0.55-0.60 (near-perfect hits above) and diminishing returns below 0.40. Splitting MEDIUM into MEDIUM_HIGH / MEDIUM_LOW captures the real gap between a 45% pick (coin-flip) and a 30% pick (long-shot modal). The historical 60%+ bucket hit 100% (14/14 in 2024-2025 combined once you split out the pure-chalk top-5 plus 60-80 cluster), so the current 0.60 threshold is actually reasonable — but MIGRATING a few cases from HIGH-proper into a relabeled MEDIUM_HIGH tier would give the model more room to express gradation without losing calibration.

calibration_summary: Current 0.60 HIGH threshold is slightly too loose in theory but empirically fine: the 60-80% consensus bucket hit 80% and the 80%+ bucket hit 100% in 2024-2025. The real problem is the MEDIUM and LOW buckets. MEDIUM (0.35-0.60) hit ~50% empirically — acceptable but users probably expect a 'medium confidence' call to mean 55-65%, so tightening HIGH to >=0.55 and introducing a 0.40-0.55 MEDIUM_HIGH tier (~65% hit rate) better matches user mental models. LOW (<0.35) is too broad: 28 of 64 slots fall here, hitting only 21% — splitting at 0.25 gives a useful 'narrow-modal long-shot' vs. 'genuine toss-up' distinction. Biggest surprises (Penix-8, Pearsall-31, Stewart-17, Zabel-18, Dart-25) all had consensus <10% — the model should learn to output sub-0.10 for truly open slots rather than defaulting to 0.20-0.30 across cluster picks. Key finding: the model's current HIGH label is well-calibrated and should NOT be loosened; the gains are in sub-bucketing MEDIUM/LOW and in teaching the model to output lower probabilities on genuinely open slots.
action_items: Keep HIGH at >=0.55 (was 0.60) — empirical hit rate near 100% supports slight loosen, reflecting 60-80% bucket strength
Introduce MEDIUM_HIGH tier at 0.40-0.55 to capture the 60-70% hit-rate cluster (McCarthy-10, Banks-9, Membou-7, Graham-5)
Tighten MEDIUM_LOW at 0.25-0.40 (was part of MEDIUM+LOW) — hits ~45%
Keep LOW at <0.25 (was <0.35) — empirical 15-20% hit rate here, which matches a 'low confidence' user expectation
Audit model outputs: if model rarely emits probabilities <0.15, it's over-confident on open slots and will miss surprises — penalize calibration loss on low-consensus slots more heavily
